text: Day 3 at BigCorp on the design system team. So many meetings just to change a button radius. There has to be a better way to ship.
Spent the weekend rebuilding our component library in Storybook. 400 components, 0 docs. Pray for me.
@devpeer_demo honestly the framework wars are a distraction. ship something. anything.
Hot take: most 'scalability' problems are imaginary. You will not have a million users. Build for the ten you have.
Quit BigCorp today. Three years of process. I want to feel the whole loop again — idea to user — without a Jira ticket in between.
First indie app shipped in a weekend. A tiny offline notes thing. 12 users. I have never been happier.
The cloud was a mistake for small apps. Your data should live on your device. Sync is a feature, not a foundation.
local-first isn't nostalgia. it's the only way to build software that respects the person using it. own your data, own your tools.
@devpeer_demo the moat was never the tech. it's having a point of view. generic tools are already free.
Shipping in public changed everything for me. Every bug fix is a tweet. Every user is a conversation. The build IS the marketing.
Crossed $2k MRR on the notes app today. From 12 users to 800 paying in 8 months. Small and sharp beats big and generic, every time.
I keep meeting devs waiting to feel 'ready' to go indie. You don't get ready first. You start, and the readiness shows up later.
Honestly torn: I preach 'build for the ten users you have' but I refresh my MRR chart ten times a day. Growth brain is hard to quit.
Two years out of BigCorp. The lesson that stuck: software is just a way to hand someone your point of view. Make it personal or don't bother.
